title: Foundations of IT and Core Hardware Components
description:
This course features Coursera Coach!
A smarter way to learn with interactive, real-time conversations that help you test your knowledge, challenge assumptions, and deepen your understanding as you progress through the course.

Embark on a comprehensive journey through the world of IT and core hardware components in this foundational course. You will gain an in-depth understanding of key IT concepts and the critical hardware components that power modern computers. By the end of the course, you will be equipped with the knowledge and skills needed to pursue an entry-level career in IT, starting with the renowned CompTIA A+ certification.

The course begins with a promotional overview of the CompTIA A+ certification, followed by an introduction to the exam's structure. From there, you’ll dive into various components, including CPUs, RAM, BIOS/UEFI, and motherboards. Each section is designed to walk you through critical topics, such as choosing the right hardware, installation techniques, and troubleshooting strategies. You'll also explore real-world scenarios with practical advice on system diagnostics and repairs, ensuring you're prepared for the certification exams and your future IT career.

This course is ideal for individuals aiming to enter IT and those looking to strengthen their foundational knowledge. No prior experience is required, but a basic understanding of computers will be helpful. The course is suitable for beginners looking to obtain the CompTIA A+ certification.

By the end of the course, you will be able to identify and describe the core components of a computer, install and troubleshoot hardware components, explain the purpose of various technologies like CPUs and RAM, and confidently prepare for the CompTIA A+ certification exams.
